title: Smoky Juicy Egyptian Chicken Curry
description: A hearty, Mediterranean-friendly chicken curry infused with Thai red curry paste, smoked paprika, and a touch of lime for brightness.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, and coriander; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and Thai red curry paste; stir to coat the vegetables and let the sauce thicken for 4 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the chicken pieces, season with salt, and cook, covered, for 12–15 minutes or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender.
Squeeze fresh lime juice into the curry, stir, and taste, adjusting salt if needed.
Transfer the curry to a baking dish, cover loosely with foil, and broil in the oven at 200°C (400°F) for 3–4 minutes to caramelize the top and enhance smokiness.
Let the dish rest for 2 minutes, then garnish with fresh cilantro if desired before serving.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with crusty bread for a satisfying solo dinner.
